brahma-sahasranama: Contains thousand names of Lord Brahma the Creator
Extremely rare Sahasranama dedicated to Brahma
Invokes all aspects of Brahma as Creator, Prajapati, Virinchi
Invokes supreme creative power and knowledge of all vedas
Sung on Brahma Jayanti and during Thursday worship of the Creator
budha-stotram: Strengthens a weak Budha (Mercury) in the birth chart
Sharpens intellect, speech, and skill in trade and commerce
Steadies the mind for study, analysis, and clear communication
Calms Budha's malefic effects on learning and business dealings
Recited on Wednesdays and Budh Purnima to honour Mercury
chandra-stotram: Strengthens an afflicted Chandra (Moon) in the horoscope
Soothes emotional turbulence and brings mental calm
Settles the restless mind and supports sound sleep and health
Eases the Moon's malefic phases and emotional distress
Ideal for Chandra graha shanti and worship
chardham-stotram: Honours the four sacred dhams of Badrinath, Dwarka, Puri, and Rameswaram
Channels the grace of Vishnu, Shiva, and Krishna of the four shrines
Brings the merit of pilgrimage to those unable to travel
Recited to wash away sins and quicken the path to moksha
Suited to Devuthani Ekadashi, Rath Yatra, and pilgrimage days
dakshinamurthy-pancharatnam: Five gem-verses by Shankaracharya on Shiva as the silent Guru
Awakens Self-knowledge and the teaching transmitted in silence
Dispels the ignorance that veils the inner Atman
Recited to advance toward Self-realisation and moksha
Sung on Guru Purnima and Maha Shivaratri before the Guru-form of Shiva
daridrya-dahana-shiva-stotram: Specifically destroys poverty (Daridrya) and associated sorrows
Calls on Shiva to burn away want and grant lasting abundance
Clears financial obstacles, mounting debts, and money worries
Daily recitation brings steady improvement in financial condition
Traditionally recited for removal of bad karma that causes poverty
Invokes brahmaloka and ultimate peace to the devoted reciter
Especially powerful when recited on Mondays and during Shravan
dashavatara-stotram: Praises all ten avatars of Vishnu from Matsya to Kalki
Invokes the combined blessings of all divine incarnations
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and bestows auspiciousness
Traditionally associated with joy and spiritual fulfillment
One of the most celebrated hymns in Indian devotional literature
Invokes liberation through understanding of divine descents
dattatreya-ashtottara-shatanamavali: Perfect for archana worship offering 108 names to Dattatreya
Traditionally recited for removal of kalimala (impurities of kali yuga)
Invokes the blessing of bhakti, mukti, siddhi, buddhi, and aishvarya
Offered on Datta Jayanti and Thursdays for the Guru's grace
dattatreya-hridayam: Heart hymn of Lord Dattatreya, the Trimuriti Guru
Invokes the combined power of Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva
Invokes guru's grace, knowledge, and liberation
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases and calamities
Recited on Datta Jayanti and Guru Purnima for the Avadhuta's blessing
dattatreya-pancharatnam: Five gem-verses adoring Dattatreya, the Avadhuta Guru
Calls on Datta as the union of Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva
Draws the Guru's protective grace over the seeker
Recited to dissolve karmic bondage and aid the path to moksha
Sung on Datta Jayanti, Guru Purnima, and Thursdays
dattatreya-upanishad-key-verses: Contains essential teachings of Dattatreya Upanishad
Describes the 24 gurus of Dattatreya from nature
Frees the seeker from the cycle of birth and rebirth
Deepens understanding of Atman through Dattatreya's teachings
devi-bhujanga-stotram: Invokes Parashakti in her Mahishasuramardini form
Traditionally recited for removal of all fears, obstacles, and calamities
Invokes sarvartha-siddhi — fulfillment of all desires
Composed in melodious Bhujangaprayata metre by Shankaracharya
Invokes the blessing of courage, protection, and divine grace of devi
Sung during Navaratri and Durga Puja to invoke the Mother
durga-ashtottara-shatanamavali: Reciting 108 names of Durga bestows complete protection of the Divine Mother
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies, obstacles, and evil forces
Instills courage, inner strength, and freedom from fear
Used during Navratri puja, Durga Ashtami, and Kumari Puja
Particularly powerful during Navratri and on Tuesdays and Fridays
durga-gayatri: Three Durga Gayatri variations — Katyayani, Mahadevi, and Girija
Shields the devotee from calamity, danger, and sudden harm
Powerful protection mantra for Navratri and Tuesdays/Fridays
Drives off enemies and negative forces through the Mother's might
Invokes the blessing of courage, strength, and divine mother's grace
Leads the devotee to Devi Loka
durga-mangalashtakam: Invokes the nine forms of Durga through auspicious verses
Wards off calamity and danger with the Mother's mangala blessing
Covers all Navadurga forms in mangala format
Ideal for Navratri, Durga Puja, and Fridays
Drives away enemies and clears obstacles from the life path
Invokes the blessing of courage, protection, and divine mother's grace
durga-suktam: Vedic hymn to Goddess Durga for crossing all difficulties
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and dangers from life's path
Invokes divine protection like a boat crossing an ocean
Recited to repel enemies and the negative forces that beset the home
Invokes the blessing of courage to face the most difficult situations
Traditionally associated with auspiciousness and good fortune to family
Includes the Durga Gayatri for complete invocation
dwadasha-jyotirlinga-stotram: Names all twelve Jyotirlingas from Somnath to Ghrishneshwar in one hymn
Carries Shiva's grace from every sacred Jyotirlinga shrine
Brings the merit of darshan at all twelve lingas to the reciter
Recited to wash away sins and grant peace of mind
Sung on Maha Shivaratri and during Monday Shiva worship
ganesh-dwadash-nama: Contains two sets of 12 sacred names of Ganesha for maximum blessings
Recitation before education, marriage, and all important tasks removes obstacles
Invokes knowledge, fame, and domestic happiness
Morning recitation makes the entire world favorable to the devotee
Invokes the blessing of all types of wealth and prosperity
Considered the most auspicious among auspicious prayers
ganesha-ashtottara-shatanamavali: Offers 108 names of Ganesha for archana and formal puja
Calls on the Vighnaharta to clear obstacles before any task
Draws Ganesha's grace for success in new beginnings
Recited to wash away faults and earn spiritual merit
Used in Ganesh Chaturthi worship and on Wednesdays
ganesha-hridayam: Heart hymn revealing the inner essence of Lord Ganesha
Invokes Ganesha as the remover of every obstacle
Surrounds the devotee with Ganesha's protective grace
Recited to purify the mind and gain his merit
Suited to Ganesh Chaturthi and Wednesday worship
ganesha-mangalashtakam: Eight auspicious verses showering Ganesha's mangala blessings
Invokes Vighnaharta to dissolve obstacles before undertakings
Brings auspicious beginnings and good fortune to the home
Recited to remove faults and grant success in endeavours
Sung on Ganesh Chaturthi and during Wednesday worship
ganga-chalisa: Invokes the purifying blessings of Mother Ganga
Washes away all sins (patit pavani effect)
Invokes moksha and liberation from the cycle of rebirth
Provides merit equivalent to bathing in the Ganga
Traditionally associated with peace to departed ancestors (pitru tarpan benefit)
Recited on Purnima and Amavasya for Ganga's grace and pitru relief
Purifies the home when recited with Gangajal present
ganga-stotram: Composed by Adi Shankaracharya - one of the holiest Ganga prayers
Cleanses the devotee of every sin through Ganga's purifying grace
Liberates from the cycle of birth and death
Devotees of Ganga remain beyond the reach of Yama (death)
Invokes the merit of residing at ganga's banks
Invokes the blessing of moksha and removes all sorrows, diseases, and fears
garuda-dandakam: Traditionally recited for removal of fear of snakes and serpent afflictions (sarpa dosha)
Neutralizes all types of poison and venom
Traditionally recited for removal of all diseases and ailments
Traditionally invoked for protection from enemies and evil spirits
Invokes the protection of Garuda, Vishnu's divine vehicle
Builds courage and frees the heart from fear and dread
gayatri-kavacham: Wraps the devotee in Gayatri's protective armour against all harm
Protects all body parts and vital energies (pranas)
Students gain knowledge and academic success
Traditionally recited for removal of all accumulated sins
Invokes ultimate liberation when recited at three sandhyas daily
